what_it_does: Defines a static 1600×900 HTML/CSS design slide showing two large warm-neutral cards with nested stat boxes, explanatory fragments, blueprint corner texture, a centered title, a small V mark, and a reduced footer stripe.
observations: Single-file HTML document with embedded CSS and no script.
Loads Plus Jakarta Sans from Google Fonts and shared variables from /surface/styles/vars.css.
Uses a fixed-size .view centered in the browser body.
Creates faint blueprint-grid textures in the top-left and top-right corners with masked background layers.
Main content is a two-column grid of cards labeled Instrument & Terms and Outlook.
Nested stat boxes use lighter surfaces, soft inset highlights, and gold or charcoal numeric text.
The original footer band markup remains, but later CSS overrides reduce it to a thin patterned strip and hide its children.
The .mark element is a small circular V badge positioned near the lower right.
standouts: Near-white surface stacking is the central visual device.
CSS comments encode design goals such as blueprint corners, footer grounding, warm-neutral surfaces, and lighter nested boxes.
A v2.1 correction block overrides earlier footer and mark behavior with !important rules.
conventions: Uses CSS custom properties extensively for color, spacing, radius, and type scales.
Keeps styling file-local inside a style tag while depending on shared variable definitions.
Uses semantic-light div structure focused on visual layout rather than document hierarchy.
Breaks responsive conventions by fixing the artboard at 1600×900.
concerns: Duplicate .gband and .mark rules rely on later overrides and !important declarations.
The fixed 1600×900 viewport may overflow or scale poorly outside the intended presentation frame.
External font and variable stylesheet dependencies are required for the design to render as intended.
Hidden footer copy remains in the DOM while being visually suppressed.
notes: Briefing remark: this file is a static design substrate for nested warm-neutral plates, soft depth, and restrained gold accenting.
questions: Is the hidden footer content intentionally retained for provenance or should it be removed from the markup?
Is the fixed 1600×900 frame required by the surrounding presentation system?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the title, visible content, and CSS comments all point to a nested-plates/proportion study using stacked near-white surfaces and light-based depth.
intention_signals: The file seems to be trying to demonstrate depth and hierarchy through subtle surface differences, soft shadows, and nested stat boxes while keeping decorative elements quiet and file-local.
novelty: The composition includes an explicit correction layer that leaves the earlier footer design in place but visually converts it into a minimal patterned foot stripe.
contribution: Contributes a two-card nested-plate layout, light-toned stat containers, blueprint-corner atmosphere, subdued footer grounding, and CSS-commented design rationale that a fused system could retain as a warm-neutral depth treatment.
summary_for_embedding: Static HTML/CSS presentation slide with a fixed 1600×900 artboard, two warm-neutral cards, nested stat boxes, blueprint corner texture, title, V mark, and thin patterned footer stripe.
intention_for_embedding: Designed as a visual study of nested near-white surfaces, proportion, restrained accent color, and depth created through light and subtle elevation.
